source_inputs.use_in_this_artifact: claim ids, causal logic, public routing policy, and forbidden empty claims
per-slide thesis, proof object, visual role, on-canvas copy, and note/viewer routing
forbidden empty claims and capability-specific quality boundaries
reader questions, proof obligations, and public-surface specificity requirements
narrative proof objects, note routes, and viewer-only proof boundaries

artifact_scope.goal: Audit the six-slide story for content density before the scene compiler turns it into renderable structure.
artifact_scope.quality_gate: Every slide must retain a main claim, proof object, business logic, visual role, on-canvas copy decision, and delete-or-note route.

audit_policy.empty_claim: A slide fails if it could be read as a generic design-quality slogan after removing the product name.
audit_policy.duplicate_message: A slide fails if its role, audience question, and product claim repeat another slide without advancing the story arc.
audit_policy.claim_without_proof: A slide fails if its main claim does not carry a named proof object and visible evidence.
audit_policy.copy_visual_mismatch: A slide fails if the visual role does not show the product mechanism or proof object named in copy.
audit_policy.public_surface_routing: A slide fails if raw trace, schema, source inventory, release metadata, or full proof lists appear as public slide copy.
audit_policy.text_budget_fit: A slide fails if the required on-canvas copy cannot fit the role's public-word and proof-label budget.

quality_checks.evidence: The claim names Vulca, source-bound generation, a commercial brief, and editable PPT output.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep product identity and output format on canvas.

quality_checks.evidence: The cover owns product identity; later slides own compiler mechanism, contrast, proof, result, and release gate.
quality_checks.route_decision: Do not reuse the cover as a generic launch slogan on later slides.

quality_checks.evidence: The proof object is the input-to-output product surface with brief, source memory, editable preview, and review boundary.
quality_checks.route_decision: Render the path as the proof object, not as decoration.

quality_checks.evidence: The visual role is the opening product path and matches the brief-to-editable-PPT claim.
quality_checks.route_decision: Delete decorative hero imagery if it hides the product path.

quality_checks.evidence: Raw source records, review metadata, and full proof fields are routed to viewer-only material.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep trace language off canvas and in the note/viewer route.

quality_checks.evidence: Headline, supporting line, and four proof labels fit inside the 58-word cover budget.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep public copy short so the product path carries the proof.

watch_items: Keep the review boundary visible so the cover does not imply public release.

approved_content_units.main_claim: Vulca is a source-bound system that turns a commercial brief and selected source memory into a code-generated editable PPT deck.
approved_content_units.business_logic: The product is credible only if the viewer can connect the real brief, allowed memory, generated editable output, and review boundary in one glance.

on_canvas_copy.headline: A real brief becomes editable PPT proof.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Commercial brief, curated source memory, and code-generated editable PPT appear as one product path.

must_delete_from_canvas: decorative hero with no product object
delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: State that this is not an image-only demo; the product promise is editable PPT with inspectable inputs.

scene_compiler_constraints.must_keep_main_claim: Vulca is a source-bound system that turns a commercial brief and selected source memory into a code-generated editable PPT deck.

quality_checks.evidence: The claim explains the compiler stage: source observations become capability memory, message obligations, and proof objects before rendering.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep the compiler mechanism, not a vague data-workflow promise.

quality_checks.evidence: The setup advances from input boundary to understanding layer, which the cover only names.
quality_checks.route_decision: Do not repeat the cover's input-to-output path.

quality_checks.evidence: The proof object is a source-to-memory compiler stage with source pack, capability memory, message obligation, and proof object.
quality_checks.route_decision: Show the compiler artifacts as evidence.

quality_checks.evidence: The visual role is a compiler path, matching the before-drawing mechanism in the headline and thesis.
quality_checks.route_decision: Delete raw tutorial transcript or copied frames from the public surface.

quality_checks.evidence: Source audit gaps, blocked weak sources, and full lineage are routed to viewer-only material.
quality_checks.route_decision: Use speaker note for why tutorials become original product memory.

quality_checks.evidence: Headline, supporting line, and four proof labels fit inside the 66-word setup budget.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep source detail compact and move raw source evidence off canvas.

watch_items: Avoid turning the compiler path into a dense audit checklist.

approved_content_units.main_claim: Allowed source observations are reduced into capability memory, message obligations, and proof objects before any slide is rendered.
approved_content_units.business_logic: A deck can only explain its design decisions if source material is compiled into product memory and obligations before drawing starts.

on_canvas_copy.headline: Sources become product memory before drawing.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Allowed source observations become capability memory, message obligations, and proof objects.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Explain that tutorials and videos guide decisions only after they are converted into original product memory.

scene_compiler_constraints.must_keep_main_claim: Allowed source observations are reduced into capability memory, message obligations, and proof objects before any slide is rendered.

quality_checks.evidence: The claim names the missing mechanisms: source memory, proof routing, mapped design moves, and editable output guarantees.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep comparison grounded in mechanism rather than taste.

quality_checks.evidence: The contrast slide uniquely explains why prompt-only and image-only routes break.
quality_checks.route_decision: Do not repeat setup mechanics except as the reason the alternate path breaks.

quality_checks.evidence: The proof object is a before-after route break with prompt-only route, missing compiler turn, Vulca route, and editable destination.
quality_checks.route_decision: Show the route break as the proof, not a competitor scorecard.

quality_checks.evidence: The visual role is route comparison, matching the thesis about missing mechanism.
quality_checks.route_decision: Delete subjective style ranking and template gallery references.

quality_checks.evidence: Competitor boundary ids, bad-control details, and raw comparison fields are routed to viewer-only material.
quality_checks.route_decision: Use public copy for mechanism, not internal comparison fields.

quality_checks.evidence: Headline, supporting line, and four proof labels fit inside the 70-word contrast budget.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep the contrast readable by limiting public proof labels to four mechanisms.

watch_items: Keep the bad-control route objective and avoid style-only superiority language.

approved_content_units.main_claim: Prompt-only can produce plausible slides, but it lacks source memory, proof routing, mapped design moves, and editable output guarantees.
approved_content_units.business_logic: The product difference is credible when the alternate path fails at a named missing mechanism and the Vulca path continues to editable output.

on_canvas_copy.headline: The difference is mechanism, not taste.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Vulca binds source memory, proof routing, design moves, and editable output before the deck is claimed.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Name prompt-only and image-only paths as missing mechanism, not as inferior style by default.

scene_compiler_constraints.must_keep_main_claim: Prompt-only can produce plausible slides, but it lacks source memory, proof routing, mapped design moves, and editable output guarantees.

quality_checks.evidence: The claim states the trust model: compact public proof markers point to inspectable workflow evidence.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep proof names specific enough to avoid generic trust language.

quality_checks.evidence: The proof slide uniquely explains how the viewer knows the workflow was used.
quality_checks.route_decision: Do not duplicate the contrast slide's route break.

quality_checks.evidence: The proof object is a compact inspection board with capability binding, contract match, content check, and bad-control failure.
quality_checks.route_decision: Render proof markers and route raw evidence to viewer-only material.

quality_checks.evidence: The visual role is an inspection board, matching the public proof-marker claim.
quality_checks.route_decision: Delete schema reports, raw proof lists, and oversized compliance tables from the slide.

quality_checks.evidence: Reader question records, proof obligations, and bad-control comparison details are routed to viewer-only material.
quality_checks.route_decision: Use the speaker note to say raw proof remains inspectable off canvas.

quality_checks.evidence: Headline, supporting line, and four proof labels fit inside the 76-word proof budget.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep four named proof states and avoid raw fields on canvas.

watch_items: Keep the inspection board compact so proof does not become the whole presentation.

approved_content_units.main_claim: Each public proof marker points to capability binding, contract match, content check, and bad-control failure behind the surface.
approved_content_units.business_logic: Trust improves when public proof is readable but still points to off-canvas records that can be inspected.

on_canvas_copy.headline: Proof stays inspectable without taking over the slide.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Compact proof markers show that the workflow consumed content, contracts, and comparison boundaries.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Explain that raw proof stays inspectable off-canvas so the public slide remains readable.

scene_compiler_constraints.must_keep_main_claim: Each public proof marker points to capability binding, contract match, content check, and bad-control failure behind the surface.

quality_checks.evidence: The claim names the convergence of design memory, mapped moves, code generation, editable preview, and QA proof.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep the result object as the main product memory.

quality_checks.evidence: The climax uniquely converts prior mechanism and proof into one remembered result.
quality_checks.route_decision: Do not repeat setup or proof as equal cards.

quality_checks.evidence: The proof object is an editable PPT preview hero object with route, renderer tag, editable preview, and QA proof tag.
quality_checks.route_decision: Attach proof to the result object instead of separating it into reports.

quality_checks.evidence: The visual role is hero editable result, matching the one-result thesis.
quality_checks.route_decision: Delete equal-weight report cards or disconnected diagrams.

quality_checks.evidence: Full product term list, source refs, and internal QA criteria are routed to viewer-only material.
quality_checks.route_decision: Use the speaker note to protect the cinematic product moment from proof clutter.

quality_checks.evidence: Headline, supporting line, and four proof labels fit inside the 62-word climax budget.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep the copy supportive so the editable preview dominates.

watch_items: Do not let orbiting proof tags compete with the editable preview.

approved_content_units.main_claim: The brief, source memory, mapped design moves, code renderer, editable preview, and QA proof converge into one result object.
approved_content_units.business_logic: The product becomes memorable when the user sees the operating loop converge into one editable artifact rather than disconnected process cards.

on_canvas_copy.headline: One editable result owns the product moment.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Design memory, mapped design moves, code generation, and proof converge into one editable PPT preview.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Keep this slide cinematic and least report-like; proof should orbit the result, not compete with it.

scene_compiler_constraints.must_keep_main_claim: The brief, source memory, mapped design moves, code renderer, editable preview, and QA proof converge into one result object.

quality_checks.evidence: The claim names the remaining proof and review gates instead of using launch-ready language.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep the next action concrete and the release boundary explicit.

quality_checks.evidence: The close uniquely converts content readiness into a gated handoff for the next generated proof.
quality_checks.route_decision: Do not re-sell the climax result as a finished release.

quality_checks.evidence: The proof object is a release handoff gate with story ready, next generated proof, human review, and source-boundary review.
quality_checks.route_decision: Show the gate rather than claiming the product is already public-ready.

quality_checks.evidence: The visual role is a quiet release decision wall, matching the blocked-release thesis.
quality_checks.route_decision: Delete launch celebration or dense run history from the public surface.

quality_checks.evidence: Run-specific handoff ids, full release checklist, and human approval notes are routed to viewer-only material.
quality_checks.route_decision: Use the speaker note for the remaining gates.

quality_checks.evidence: Headline, supporting line, and four proof labels fit inside the 60-word close budget.
quality_checks.route_decision: Keep the close quiet and decisive rather than turning it into an appendix.

watch_items: Keep release blocked language visible enough that the content story is not mistaken for approval.

approved_content_units.main_claim: The story can feed the next generated deck, but public release waits for visual review, native inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.
approved_content_units.business_logic: Credibility is preserved when the deck names the next proof and review gate instead of turning content readiness into a release claim.

on_canvas_copy.headline: The next proof is generated, not claimed.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: This story becomes input for the next generated deck, then waits for review before release.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Name the remaining gate calmly: generated proof, visual review, native inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.

scene_compiler_constraints.must_keep_main_claim: The story can feed the next generated deck, but public release waits for visual review, native inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.
